Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> 
> do (or should) we need to do the same for the other components?

It would be awesome if you could do this for other components as it would help with k8s v1.16+ migration
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> > 
> > do (or should) we need to do the same for the other components?
> 
> It would be awesome if you could do this for other components as it would help with k8s v1.16+ migration

Ok. The idea was to do all the back and forth for one component only.
Now that this change it completed and stabilized, I suggest to have it merged and then we'll have another one to apply the same for other remaining policy services. POLICY-2594 is created for that.
When is the K8s 1.16 migration planned for?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3:
> 
> > Patch Set 3:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> > > 
> > > do (or should) we need to do the same for the other components?
> > 
> > It would be awesome if you could do this for other components as it would help with k8s v1.16+ migration
> 
> Ok. The idea was to do all the back and forth for one component only.
> Now that this change it completed and stabilized, I suggest to have it merged and then we'll have another one to apply the same for other remaining policy services. POLICY-2594 is created for that.

I fully agree with you. Smaller patches are easier to debug and to review. I'm just giving the floor to @Sylvain to review this as he is our service template expert;)

> When is the K8s 1.16 migration planned for?

We hope to do this in G but there is quite a lot of work before this is possible so we will see if we make it.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+2

First, thank you for the patch!
If you want to continue on doing this on policy, it would be awesome (and one patch per subcomponent is good as the review is small, as my brain ;) )
Fyi, I'm working on other templates in order to ease some work and VID gerrit is the "showroom" of them (https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/107749)
Notice that "common.podSecurityContext" and "common.containerSecurityContext" may lead to error as it prevents write on rootfs.

So feel free to use them on other patches or I'll do that later on.
Change has been successfully merged by Sylvain Desbureaux
